skill.name: video-enhancement
skill.description: AI Video Enhancement - Upscale video resolution, improve quality, denoise, sharpen, enhance low-quality videos to HD/4K. Supports local video files, remote URLs (YouTube, Bilibili), auto-download, real-time progress tracking.
skill.summary: Enhance and upscale video quality using AI with support for local files and remote URLs
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives representing legitimate functionality for a video processing tool. Network requests are made to documented verging.ai API endpoints. External commands (ffmpeg, ffprobe, curl, yt-dlp) are standard video processing tools with hardcoded arguments. Environment variables store API keys as documented. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns detected.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution
low_findings.description: Skill uses ffmpeg, ffprobe, curl, and yt-dlp commands for video processing. All commands shown in documentation use hardcoded arguments with no user input injection. This is legitimate video processing tooling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation shows standard video processing commands with no evidence of user-controlled input or injection vectors

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTP requests to verging.ai API endpoints for video processing services. All URLs are hardcoded to legitimate service endpoints documented in the skill README.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network requests are to documented verging.ai API endpoints for legitimate video enhancement services

low_findings.title: API Key in Environment Variable
low_findings.description: Skill reads VERGING_API_KEY environment variable for authentication with verging.ai API. This is the standard, secure pattern for API credential management.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard practice for API credentials - no exfiltration or misuse detected

low_findings.title: Temporary File System Access
low_findings.description: Skill uses /tmp/verging-video-enhancement/ directory for temporary video storage during processing. This is standard behavior for video processing workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate use of temp directory for video processing with documented cleanup behavior

content.value_statement: Transform low-resolution videos into HD or 4K quality using AI-powered upscaling. Automatically denoise, sharpen, and improve video clarity for professional results.
actual_capabilities: Upscale video resolution from low quality to HD or 4K using AI
Download videos from remote sources including YouTube and Bilibili
Trim videos to specific time ranges before processing
Apply AI denoising and sharpening to improve clarity
Process videos in normal or HD quality modes
Track job progress with real-time status updates
limitations: Maximum video duration is 30 seconds per job
Requires verging.ai API key and credits for processing
HD mode consumes 3 credits per second vs 1 credit for normal mode
Depends on external tools: ffmpeg, ffprobe, curl, and yt-dlp-downloader-skill for remote videos

use_cases.title: Restore Old Family Videos
use_cases.description: Enhance vintage or low-resolution home videos to modern quality standards. Upscale, denoise, and sharpen footage for better viewing on current displays.
use_cases.target_user: Home users with vintage video collections

use_cases.title: Content Creator Video Optimization
use_cases.description: Improve video quality before publishing to social media or video platforms. Use HD mode for professional-quality upscaling of lower resolution source footage.
use_cases.target_user: Video creators and social media publishers

use_cases.title: Surveillance Footage Enhancement
use_cases.description: Clarify and enhance security camera footage for better detail visibility. Process specific time segments to focus on critical events.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Enhancement
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance the video quality of my video file at ./old-video.mp4
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple local video enhancement with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Enhance YouTube Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Download and enhance the video from https://youtube.com/watch?v=example with HD quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Remote video download and enhancement with higher quality mode

prompt_templates.title: Enhance Video Segment
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance video.mp4 from second 5 to second 15 and save the result locally
prompt_templates.scenario: Process specific time range with local download

prompt_templates.title: Batch Enhancement with Custom Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance all videos in the ./videos folder using normal mode and save results to ./enhanced-output
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple video processing with custom output directory

output_examples.input: Enhance https://youtube.com/watch?v=example from 0 to 10 seconds in HD mode
output: Downloading video from YouTube using yt-dlp...

best_practices: Trim videos to the specific segment you need before enhancement to save processing time and credits
Use normal mode for most videos; only use HD mode when you need maximum quality improvement
Check your credit balance before processing long videos to ensure sufficient funds
Test enhancement on a short video segment first before processing the full video
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to enhance videos longer than 30 seconds as the job will fail
Do not process videos without sufficient credits as the job will not start
Do not forget to install yt-dlp-downloader-skill if processing remote video URLs
Do not expose your VERGING_API_KEY in public repositories or share it with others

faq.question: What is the maximum video length I can enhance?
faq.answer: The maximum video duration is 30 seconds. For longer videos, use the --start and --end options to process specific segments.

faq.question: How much does it cost to enhance videos?
faq.answer: Normal mode costs 1 credit per second of video. HD mode costs 3 credits per second. A 10-second video costs 10 credits in normal mode or 30 credits in HD mode.

faq.question: Do I need to install additional tools?
faq.answer: You need ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl installed. For remote videos (YouTube, Bilibili), you also need the yt-dlp-downloader-skill installed via clawhub.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, and WebM formats are supported. Maximum file size is 500MB.

faq.question: Where do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit https://verging.ai, log in, click your avatar, and select API Keys. Create a new key and set it as the VERGING_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can I download the enhanced video?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --download flag to automatically download the result. Enhanced videos are also available via a public URL returned after processing.
